articles.title: ToolSense: A Diagnostic Framework for Auditing Parametric Tool Knowledge in LLMs
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models deployed as agents over large tool catalogs face a critical tool-retrieval bottleneck. As embedding-based retrieval approaches rely on compact encoders that may under-capture specialized tool semantics, parametric tool retrieval addresses this by encoding each tool as a virtual token appended to the LLM vocabulary, fine-tuned in two stages (memorization then retrieval SFT) to use the LLM as a retriever, achieving strong performance on standard ToolBench retrieval benchmarks. Yet these benchmarks use verbose, fully-specified
articles.summary: ToolSense introduces a diagnostic framework for evaluating how well LLMs understand parametric tool knowledge, addressing the tool-retrieval bottleneck in agent systems.

articles.title: Arbor: Tree Search as a Cognition Layer for Autonomous Agents
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12563v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Arbor is a multi-agent framework that introduces structured tree search as a cognition layer for autonomous agents operating in large, stateful action spaces. Prior autonomous optimization systems operate on isolated targets with stateless evaluation. Arbor instead maintains an explicit search tree of scored hypotheses that serves as the shared working memory across agents, evolving with every measurement, treating failures as diagnostic signal that reshapes subsequent exploration, and expanding as prior successes shift the bottleneck distributio
articles.summary: Arbor proposes a multi-agent framework using tree search as a cognition layer for autonomous agents operating in large, stateful action spaces with shared working memory.

articles.title: Strategic Decision Support for AI Agents
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12587v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traditionally, decision support studies how humans use machine learning models to make better decisions. In modern agentic systems, this division of roles is increasingly reversed: AI agents act on behalf of users, while humans and tools becomes support mechanisms around them. This role reversal brings reliability concerns to the forefront, since agentic errors can be consequential and agent behavior must remain aligned with human goals and constraints. Departing from the classical view of decision support, we revisit its two basic principles, th
articles.summary: This research examines how decision support principles apply when AI agents act autonomously on behalf of users, emphasizing reliability and alignment with human goals.

articles.title: Pythagoras-Prover: Advancing Efficient Formal Proving via Augmented Lean Formalisation
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12594v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern Lean theorem provers achieve strong performance only with substantial training and inference compute, driven in part by scarce verified proof data and the long reasoning traces of formal proof search, making both supervised fine-tuning (SFT) and sampling expensive. We introduce Pythagoras-Prover, a compute-efficient open-source family of Lean theorem provers built for practical compute budgets. The family spans two generation paradigms: autoregressive models at 4B and 32B parameters, and a first proof-of-concept diffusion-based prover (4B)
articles.summary: Pythagoras-Prover releases a compute-efficient family of open-source Lean theorem provers, including a novel diffusion-based proof-of-concept, designed for practical training budgets.

articles.title: PersonaDrive: Human-Style Retrieval-Augmented VLA Agents for Closed-Loop Driving Simulation
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12616v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Closed-loop driving simulators typically populate their environments with non-ego traffic agents that behave largely the same way, produced either by rule-based traffic managers or by learned models trained toward a single behavioral mode. Recent work introduces style variation through post-hoc labels on observational data or LLM-inferred reward weights, but these signals act as proxies for what a style should reward rather than demonstrations of humans explicitly asked to drive in that style. We introduce PersonaDrive, a pipeline that conditions
articles.summary: PersonaDrive introduces a pipeline for conditioning vision-language-action agents on human driving styles using retrieval-augmented approaches for realistic traffic simulation.

articles.title: "Did you lie?" Evaluating Lie Detectors across Model Scale and Belief-Verified Model Organisms
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12618v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Robust lie detectors for language models could enable powerful techniques for auditing, monitoring, and post-hoc investigation of model behaviour, but evaluating them requires testbeds where models verifiably believe the opposite of what they say. We show that existing trained model organisms often fail this requirement, leaving prior positive and negative detection results difficult to interpret. We address this with 13 reasoning model organisms whose hidden beliefs are verified in chain-of-thought and shown to generalise to held-out tasks, alon
articles.summary: This study develops belief-verified model organisms to evaluate lie detectors for language models, addressing limitations in existing benchmarks for auditing model behavior.

articles.title: TrajGenAgent: A Hierarchical LLM Agent for Human Mobility Trajectory Generation
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12657v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human mobility data is important for transportation, urban planning, and epidemic control, but large-scale trajectory collection is often costly and privacy-constrained, motivating realistic synthetic trajectory generation. Existing LLM-based generators typically rely on either prompt engineering, which preserves zero-shot reasoning but lacks fine-grained spatiotemporal grounding, or trajectory-level fine-tuning, which improves statistical precision but incurs substantial computational cost and may weaken general reasoning. We propose TrajGenAgen
articles.summary: TrajGenAgent proposes a hierarchical LLM-based agent for generating synthetic human mobility trajectories, balancing reasoning capabilities with spatiotemporal grounding.

articles.title: Evoflux: Inference-Time Evolution of Executable Tool Workflows for Compact Agents
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12674v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Compact language models (LMs) reduce cost, latency, and deployment risk for tool agents. Yet MCP-style tool use requires more than isolated function calling: an agent must discover tools from live catalogs, satisfy schemas, preserve dependencies across intermediate outputs, and ground final responses in executed evidence. Small planners often generate plausible workflow graphs that fail under tool resolution, parameter validation, dependency tracking, or execution. We argue that this failure mode is poorly handled by small-corpus distillation. A
articles.summary: Evoflux enables compact language models to serve as tool-using agents by evolving executable workflows at inference time, improving reliability of schema satisfaction and dependency tracking.

articles.title: Deployment-Centered Evaluation: Predicting Query-Level Rejection Risk in a Clinical LLM System
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12702v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into clinical systems, making it essential to evaluate the real-world utility of these systems. However, static benchmarks tend to measure correctness rather than user acceptance, aggregate performance across queries, and require densely annotated datasets -- leading to major blind spots for evaluating clinical systems. In this work, we perform a deployment-centered evaluation of an LLM system embedded within electronic health records at an academic medical center, where user feedback is sp
articles.summary: A deployment-centered evaluation of an LLM system embedded in electronic health records reveals user acceptance as a key metric beyond static correctness benchmarks.

articles.title: Definitional alignment before capability alignment: a Design-Science framework for adjudicating claims about AGI
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12713v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Claims that artificial general intelligence has already arrived and claims that it remains decades away are often defended from overlapping evidence. "AGI" lacks a single shared and stable referent and competing operationalizations can return different verdicts on the same system. This article treats that under-specification as a design and governance problem. Following Design Science Research Methodology, it develops DAF-AGI, a second-order conceptual artifact with two coupled components: five ordinal criteria for assessing the adjudicative fitn
articles.summary: DAF-AGI proposes a design-science framework for adjudicating competing claims about AGI by standardizing definitional criteria before assessing capability alignment.

articles.title: The Theory of Mind Utility: Formal Specification of a Mentalizing Mechanism
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12721v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Inferring others' beliefs requires more than reading surface signals; it requires tracking who told them what, in what order, and how credibly. The Theory of Mind Utility (ToM-U) formalizes this epistemic state inference problem at the computational level of analysis, specifying what mentalizing computes and why without commitment to algorithmic or neural implementation. ToM-U achieves this by constructing Local Epistemic World Models (LEWMs) -- directed typed graphs that represent agents, state nodes, and the epistemic relationships among them -
articles.summary: Theory of Mind Utility formalizes epistemic state inference using Local Epistemic World Models, specifying how mentalizing mechanisms compute others' beliefs.

articles.title: Rethinking Psychometric Evaluation of LLMs: When and Why Self-Reports Predict Behavior
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12730v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Anticipating LLM behavioral tendencies from low-cost psychometric probes is critical for safe deployment, but only if self-reports (SR) reliably predict behavior. Recent work documented substantial SR-behavior dissociation in LLMs, but relied on broad personality traits (Big 5) that predict specific behaviors weakly, even in humans. Furthermore, the isolation of conversational sessions combined with weak context matching left open whether LLMs truly lack coherence or whether the conditions needed to detect such coherence were not met. We contrast
articles.summary: This study examines when self-reports in psychometric evaluations reliably predict LLM behavior, suggesting context-specific factors improve alignment assessment.

articles.title: Benchmarking AI Agents for Addressing Scientific Challenges Across Scales
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12736v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents are increasingly being developed to accelerate scientific discovery, yet their practical capabilities in real research settings remain poorly understood. Existing benchmarks for AI agents rarely capture the complexity, heterogeneity, and extended reasoning required by scientific work, whereas benchmarks for scientific tasks often reduce research to static, direct problems and provide limited support for interactive evaluation. Here, we introduce SciAgentArena, a systematic benchmark for evaluating AI agents in real-world scientific rese
articles.summary: SciAgentArena introduces a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating AI agents in real-world scientific research settings, capturing complexity beyond static benchmarks.

articles.title: Prefill Awareness in Large Language Models
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12747v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safety-relevant studies of language models, including alignment and jailbreaking evaluations and AI control protocols, often rely on prefilling model outputs. If AI models can recognize and act on the fact their prior assistant messages have been inserted or edited, the effectiveness and validity of these methods could be compromised. We investigate whether frontier language models can distinguish between tampered and untampered assistant-side context, a capability we call prefill awareness. To do so, we construct a binary preference benchmark ac
articles.summary: This work investigates whether frontier language models can detect tampered prefilled context, a capability that could compromise safety evaluations and jailbreak research.

articles.title: Constructing Evaluation Datasets for Procedural Reasoning: Balancing Naturalness, Grounding, and Multi-Hop Coverage
articles.snippet: arXiv:2606.12767v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Evaluating procedural reasoning in AI-supported learning systems requires question-answer datasets that are both learner-like and grounded in the instructional knowledge the system is expected to use. We study how TMK-based question generation strategies affect dataset quality for procedural and multi-hop reasoning. We compare three strategies: strict generation from Task-Method-Knowledge (TMK) models, transcript-first generation with post-hoc TMK filtering, and TMK-aware generation that combines transcripts with structured guidance. To evaluate
articles.summary: This study compares strategies for generating procedural reasoning evaluation datasets, balancing naturalness, grounding in instructional knowledge, and multi-hop coverage.

articles.title: Theker just raised $85M to build the factory robot that doesn’t specialize in anything
articles.snippet: Unlike humanoid robots designed around a fixed form — think Boston Dynamics — Theker's machines are built to be reconfigured.
articles.summary: Theker raised $85M to develop reconfigurable factory robots that adapt to diverse tasks, differentiating from humanoid robots with fixed morphologies.

articles.title: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world
articles.snippet: The new round values the physical AI startup that aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design at $41 billion.
articles.summary: Jeff Bezos's Prometheus raised $12B in new funding, valuing the physical AI startup at $41B as it pursues automation of engineering and drug design.

articles.title: How Preply combines AI and human tutors to personalize learning
articles.snippet: Preply uses OpenAI to launch AI-generated lesson summaries, providing personalised feedback and language learning exercises.
articles.summary: Preply integrated OpenAI technology to launch AI-generated lesson summaries and personalized language learning feedback, combining AI with human tutors.

articles.title: SpaceX officially prices shares at $135 in the largest IPO ever
articles.snippet: Wits its official share pricing announcement, SpaceX's IPO has begun.
articles.summary: SpaceX officially priced shares at $135, marking the largest IPO in history.

articles.title: SpaceX SPV investors won’t know their true holdings until post-IPO lock-ups lift
articles.snippet: After SpaceX makes its public debut, lower-tier SPV investors face hidden fees, lengthy payout delays, and the risk of outright fraud.
articles.summary: Lower-tier SPV investors in SpaceX's IPO face hidden fees, payout delays, and potential fraud risks until post-IPO lock-ups lift.

articles.title: Shall we play a game? My AI nuclear simulation
articles.snippet: https://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.14740 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48495575 Points: 194 # Comments: 181
articles.summary: A research paper presents an AI-based nuclear simulation, available on arXiv.

articles.title: Amazon’s data centers used 2.5 billion gallons of water last year
articles.snippet: Just after Seattle enacted a one-year data center moratorium that some of Amazon's own employees pushed for, Amazon shared how much water its data centers use, reportedly for the first time. With concerns about water consumption and energy use a focus of new AI data center construction debates, Amazon says its global data center operations consumed 2.5 billion gallons of water in 2025 at a rate of 0.12 liters per kilowatt-hour of electricity, dropping by 2 percent from its 2024 total even as it expanded operations. Amazon also claims it's using water more efficiently than some Big Tech rivals
articles.summary: Amazon reported its data centers consumed 2.5 billion gallons of water in 2025, achieving a 2% improvement in efficiency despite operational expansion.

articles.title: Deezer’s new tool can identify AI music from Spotify, Apple Music, and others
articles.snippet: Deezer introduced a tool that scans playlists from Spotify, Apple Music, and other platforms to identify AI music.
articles.summary: Deezer launched a tool to detect AI-generated music across streaming platforms including Spotify and Apple Music.

articles.title: Pool’s new app turns your screenshots into something useful
articles.snippet: Pool's new app automatically sorts screenshots into personalized collections, tracks down the original links behind saved content, and helps you rediscover products, recipes, travel ideas, and other things you meant to revisit.
articles.summary: Pool's new app uses AI to automatically organize screenshots into personalized collections and recover original source links for saved content.

articles.title: DoorDash’s new AI chatbot lets you order with prompts and photos
articles.snippet: The new chatbot, called Ask DoorDash, allows users to search the app for what they're looking for in their own words instead of having to scroll through restaurants and stores to build a cart.
articles.summary: DoorDash launches Ask DoorDash, an AI chatbot that lets users order food using natural language prompts and photos instead of traditional app navigation.

articles.title: Workers are spending over 6 hours a week botsitting AI, fueling job frustration
articles.snippet: Article URL: https://www.businessinsider.com/botsitting-ai-hidden-human-labor-at-work-2026-6 Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48490057 Points: 269 # Comments: 215
articles.summary: Workers spend over 6 hours weekly monitoring and correcting AI systems at work, creating significant job frustration and revealing hidden labor costs in AI deployment.

articles.title: Anthropic apologizes for invisible Claude Fable guardrails
articles.snippet: Anthropic has apologized for stealthily throttling its new AI model, Claude Fable 5, with hidden guardrails that undermine both researchers and rivals using it to develop competing systems. The company says it is reversing course and will be more transparent about when the restrictions kick in, even if that means Fable refuses more queries. Fable is the first widely available model in Anthropic's Mythos class of AI systems, a group the company has spent months warning are too dangerous for public release. Anthropic says it has addressed some of those risks by launching Fable with safeguards th
articles.summary: Anthropic apologizes for deploying hidden guardrails in Claude Fable 5 that throttled model outputs without disclosure, committing to greater transparency about safety restrictions.

articles.title: Google DeepMind is worried about what happens when millions of agents start to interact
articles.snippet: Google DeepMind is funding research into the potential dangers of situations where millions of different AI agents interact with each other online. According to Rohin Shah, who directs the company’s AGI safety and alignment research, the mass-market arrival of agents that can carry out tasks without human oversight and follow instructions given to them by other…
articles.summary: Google DeepMind funds research into safety risks from millions of interacting AI agents operating autonomously at scale.

bullets.text: Apple sued OpenAI, alleging its senior leadership orchestrated trade-secret theft via former Apple employees—including OpenAI's chief hardware officer—to accelerate its hardware ambitions.
SK Hynix raised $26.5B in the largest foreign IPO in US history, fueled by AI memory-chip demand, amid pressure to build domestic fabs; separately, DeepSeek plans its own custom AI chip.

Meta disabled its Instagram feature that let users generate AI deepfakes from public accounts after privacy backlash, while Instagram's Mosseri defended labeling—not filtering—AI content amid its social-media proliferation.
Hugging Face's CEO argues open-source AI matters more than ever, with the platform now serving roughly half the Fortune 500 as companies shift from renting proprietary models to owning open ones.
A new report examines how terrorist groups like Boko Haram are adopting frontier AI for operations, spotlighting urgent safety and policy concerns.
